title: Chicken Satay & Rainbow Quinoa Slaw
summary: Juicy chicken skewers glazed in a honey-soy marinade, served up with a zingy peanut dressing and crunchy rainbow slaw. It’s fresh, filling and protein-packed.

method.description: Heat the grill to medium-high. If using bamboo skewers, soak them in water for at least 30 mins.
Cut the chicken into 3cm chunks. Finely grate the garlic into a bowl, then stir through the soy sauce and honey. Season with salt and black pepper. Add the chicken to the marinade and set aside.

Thinly shred the cabbage. Julienne or coarsely grate the carrot. Pick the coriander leaves and finely slice the stems. Toss the cabbage, carrot, coriander leaves and stems with the cooked quinoa into a large bowl.

In another bowl, stir together the peanut butter, gochujang, soy sauce and honey. Squeeze in lime juice to taste and mix well. If it’s too thick, loosen with a splash of cold water until it’s drizzlable. Taste and season with salt, pepper or more lime if needed.

Thread the marinated chicken pieces onto the skewers and place them on a baking tray. Grill for 10–15 mins, flipping halfway and basting with any leftover marinade, until cooked through and lightly charred. If they’re browning too quickly, lower the tray or reduce the grill heat.
Toss the slaw with half of the peanut dressing and divide between plates. Add the chicken skewers on the side and drizzle over the remaining dressing to finish.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
